member.name: Tom Gordon
member.synopsis: Tom Gordon is the Liberal Democrat MP for Harrogate and Knaresborough , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Wheelchairs: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Whizz Kidz Youth Board's report entitled Mobility Matters: A Youth Perspective on NHS Wheelchair Services, published on 26 June 2026, what assessment he has made of the adequa
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for the provision and commissioning of local wheelchair services, based on the needs of their local population. This includes wheelchairs for adults and children. NHS England supports ICBs to commission effect...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that specialist expertise, particularly in areas such as cancer, frontline NHS delivery and patient engagement, is retained and effectively integrated as NHS Engla
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that delivering reform and maintaining performance depends on retaining the right skills, knowledge, and experience that exists across both our current workforces in NHS England and the Department.The changes proposed are not a r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds health and care research via its research operational arm the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) across England. The Department is committed to ensuring that research is inclusive and representative of the populati...

writtenQuestions.heading: TrustMark: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, if he will make reforms to TrustMark to ensure it can prevent substandard contractors operating under Government-endorsed schemes; and whether an independent review of TrustMark has been comm
writtenQuestions.answerText: TrustMark operates under a Master Licence Agreement with the Department for Business and Trade and sets requirements for scheme providers who in turn oversee registered businesses delivering work under government schemes. The Department holds an observer ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether the Government plans to establish a mandatory single quality scheme for the low carbon home upgrades market.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring high standards and strong consumer protections in the delivery of low carbon home upgrades. We recognise that the current system can be complex, with multiple organisations involved, which can create confusion for b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Insulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many homes had spray foam insulation installed under (a) the Green Homes Grant, (b) the ECO4 scheme and (c) the Great British Insulation Scheme; and of those, how many have been identifie
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department publishes statistics on measures installed under the Green Homes Grant, ECO4 and the Great British Insulation Scheme, but does not hold specific data on properties with spray foam insulation. We are aware of a small number of cases where sp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what consideration he has given to establishing a regional clinical cancer fellowship scheme led by the National Institute for Health and Care Research.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the National Institute of Health and Care Research (NIHR), the Department invests in building research expertise and capacity, specialist facilities, and support services to support research across all aspects of human health, including cancer.Sin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Insulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what the timeline is for his Department bringing forward proposals on remediation and support for homeowners adversely affected by incorrectly installed spray foam insulation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I have held extensive discussions with the spray foam industry to support homeowners affected by sales difficulties or rogue removal companies. Independent guidance has been published by the Property Care Association, and the Government will continue enga...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Housing

writtenQuestions.answerText: We are currently consulting on proposals to strengthen consumer protections for home upgrades delivered through government schemes. This includes proposals for a single complaints and case-management function to oversee remediation processes and outcomes,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Military Bases: Religious Buildings

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Single Services have identified that the main barrier to same-sex couples marrying in Armed Forces chapels is the length of the process required to register such chapels for same-sex marriage. We have streamlined the process as much as possible, but i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Respite Care: VAT

writtenQuestions.answerText: Supplies of welfare services, including the provision of respite and residential care for people with permanent disabilities and dementia, are exempt from VAT if they are supplied by eligible bodies, such as public bodies or charities.Some respite care ma...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Day Care Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that respite day care services for adults with (a) dementia and (b) disabilities are affordable.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important role that respite day care can play in supporting adults with dementia, disabled adults, and unpaid carers.Where local authorities decide to charge for the provision of care and support, including respite day care s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
